cover.label: woody crown area index
cover.sampledAreaBasis: Denominator PINNED from subplotID: the measured woody plants here are all shrubs (growthForm 'small shrub'/'single shrub'), which NEON measures in the 100 m2 nested subplots. They occupy 4 of those nests (39_100, 41_100, 48_100, 51_100), so the sampled area is 4 x 100 = 400 m2 - read from the records, not inferred from where the plants fell (the gap SRER_048 flagged).
cover.includes: live woody plants whose crown NEON actually measured (both crown diameters present)
cover.excludes: mapped plants with no crown measurement (they carry a per-species default, not a measurement, and are not summed); and cacti, which NEON measures under a separate Cactus SOP against its own sampled area
cover.notCover: Crowns overlap and each is measured from its two MAXIMUM diameters at right angles, so this is summed crown area, not ground cover. The shaded fraction of ground is lower. It is not a percentage of ground and must not be reported as one.
cover.unresolved: Small-shrub and single-shrub channels can be sampled over different nested areas; this uses the occupied 100 m2 nests uniformly. Pinning each plant to its exact nested area would need the per-record nestedSubplotID area, which this file does not resolve.

cactiMapped.note: Cacti are mapped here under SRER's site-specific exception but measured under a separate Cactus SOP, so they carry a position and no crown - they are not in the index above.

note: NEON Vegetation Structure (DP1.10098.001), plot SRER_056 - every plant is a real tagged individual at its mapped position, anchored to the true 40x40 m plot centre.
surveyNote: the nested subplots NEON sampled for shrubs here

mappedExtent.note: A 40x40 m NEON base plot samples shrubs in nested subplots, not the whole plot. The mapped plants fall where those sampled nests are. x is metres east of plot centre.

provenance.accessed_basis: built 2026-07-20 from the same SRER site download used for SRER_048; exact query date not recorded at build time
provenance.release_basis: built from a live API query rather than a pinned NEON release, so the release vintage was never captured. NEON releases are versioned and revise prior data, so these records cannot be pinned to a release after the fact.
provenance.builder: build_plot2.py (scratchpad; not committed - needs raw VST CSVs + an API token)

sibling_current_release.note: The sibling app has since pinned this product to RELEASE-2026 with an exact source digest. This prototype has NOT been rebuilt against it; its records may differ from that release.

provenance.date_field_meaning: Each plant carries ONE date: the date its tag went on (vst_mappingandtagging is a one-row-per-individual tagging table). It is NOT a measurement date and NOT a survey-visit date.
provenance.cohort_note: These are FIRST-TAG COHORTS, not re-survey campaigns. Because each individual is tagged once and then re-measured in later bouts, grouping by tag date can never show the same plant twice - zero overlap between the groups is forced by the table structure and is not evidence of turnover, recruitment, or mortality.
provenance.measurement_basis: Structural values come from each plant's LATEST vst_apparentindividual record, so condition here is approximately a recent snapshot positioned at each plant's mapped location, not its tagging-era state.
provenance.cacti_scope: NEON's standard woody protocol does not map cacti; SRER (Domain 14) carries a site-specific exception to MAP large cacti, but they are MEASURED under a separate Cactus SOP (NEON.DOC.001715) whose records do not land in vst_apparentindividual - so cacti here have a position but no structural record.

unmeasured_breakdown.note: 34 plants have no structural record: 32 cacti (out of protocol scope, see cacti_scope) and 2 woody plants that were mapped but not measured.

provenance.not_every_plant: Only 113 of 147 plants carry a structural measurement; the rest are mapped positions drawn at a species-typical size and marked with an open ring.
provenance.subplotID_pinned: Unlike SRER_048, this build carries subplotID and growthForm per measured plant, so the cover denominator is read from the records rather than inferred.
